name: Ramos Gin Fizz

spec.method_detail: Combine all except soda. Dry shake 2 minutes. Add ice, shake 10–12 minutes (or 5 minutes hard). The long shake emulsifies the cream and egg. Strain into chilled highball. Slowly top with soda — it lifts the foam above the rim.

history: Henry C. Ramos invented this at his Imperial Cabinet Saloon in New Orleans in 1888. During Mardi Gras he employed 30+ bartenders just to shake these — each shake was 12 minutes long. Governor Huey Long had a Ramos Gin Fizz demonstration staged at a NYC hotel. It's labor-intensive and not for when you're in the weeds, but it's one of the great achievements in cocktail history.
